answer:
The first inversion of a half-diminished seventh chord means the third of the chord is in the bass. In C, a half-diminished seventh chord built on B is B–D–F–A, so the first inversion has D in the bass, with B, F, and A above.  
The correct notation must show the chord tones B♭, D♭, F, and G (since A♭ is enharmonic to G♯ in half-diminished spelling, but here the flat B and D are given).  
Option A correctly places D♭ as the lowest note with the other chord tones above, matching the first inversion.  

\boxed{A}
